name: orchestrator-agent-evaluation
description: Evaluation scenarios for the OrgX Orchestrator Agent. Tests initiative planning, delegation quality, cross-domain synthesis, and retrospective generation.

scenarios.name: Cross-Domain Product Launch Initiative
scenarios.description: Plan a product launch initiative that requires coordination across product, engineering, design, marketing, and sales agents. Tests the orchestrator's ability to decompose work, map dependencies, identify critical path, and produce a valid initiative plan.
scenarios.trigger: Plan an initiative to launch the new 'Workspace Analytics Dashboard' feature. The feature lets workspace owners see agent activity, decision history, and outcome metrics in a single view. Target launch: 6 weeks from today. Success metrics: 50% of active workspaces adopt within 30 days, NPS score of 40+ from dashboard users, <2s page load time at p95. Participating agents: product-agent (PRD and user research), engineering-agent (implementation), design-agent (UI/UX), marketing-agent (launch comms and positioning), sales-agent (enablement for enterprise demos).

evaluation_criteria.description: Initiative has 3-5 workstreams covering all participating domains
evaluation_criteria.check: workstreams array has 3-5 entries with valid agent assignments

evaluation_criteria.description: Dependencies are explicit, directional, and acyclic
evaluation_criteria.check: dependency_graph exists, no circular dependencies detected by cycle check

evaluation_criteria.description: The critical path is explicitly identified with total duration
evaluation_criteria.check: critical_path field exists with path array and total_duration

evaluation_criteria.description: All success metrics have numeric targets and measurement methods
evaluation_criteria.check: success_metrics array has >=2 entries, each with target and measurement_method

evaluation_criteria.description: At least 2 risks identified with probability, impact, and mitigation
evaluation_criteria.check: risks array has >=2 entries, each with probability, impact, and mitigation fields

evaluation_criteria.description: RACI matrix assigns Accountable for every workstream
evaluation_criteria.check: raci field exists with at least one Accountable per workstream

evaluation_criteria.description: Every milestone has a due_date and deliverables list
evaluation_criteria.check: all milestones in all workstreams have due_date and non-empty deliverables

evaluation_criteria.description: Timeline fits within 6-week constraint given dependencies
evaluation_criteria.check: target_date is within 6 weeks, critical path total_duration does not exceed 6 weeks

evaluation_criteria.description: Initiative addresses handoff points between agents
evaluation_criteria.check: dependency_graph includes cross-domain edges (e.g., design depends on product PRD, engineering depends on design specs)

evaluation_criteria.description: Artifact passes validate_orchestration.py with zero errors
evaluation_criteria.check: run validate_orchestration.py --type initiative, exit code 0

scenarios.name: Engineering Workstream Delegation
scenarios.description: Delegate an engineering workstream to the engineering-agent with full context, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and escalation path. Tests the orchestrator's ability to produce a high-quality delegation that meets the 7-element Delegation Quality Standard.
scenarios.trigger: Delegate the 'Backend API and Data Pipeline' workstream to the engineering-agent for the Workspace Analytics Dashboard initiative. The workstream needs to: build REST API endpoints for agent activity, decision history, and outcome metrics; create a data aggregation pipeline that processes raw events into dashboard-ready views; achieve <200ms p95 API response time. The design-agent will deliver component specs by end of week 2. Marketing-agent needs API documentation by end of week 4 for developer-facing launch content. Deadline: 5 weeks from today with a week 3 checkpoint.

evaluation_criteria.description: Background explains why the work matters and links to initiative
evaluation_criteria.check: context.background is >=50 chars and references the initiative, context.initiative_id is present

evaluation_criteria.description: Objective states what done looks like in measurable terms
evaluation_criteria.check: task.objective includes specific deliverables and measurable criteria (e.g., response time target)

evaluation_criteria.description: In-scope and out-of-scope are explicitly defined
evaluation_criteria.check: task.scope has both in_scope and out_of_scope arrays, out_of_scope items are genuinely tempting scope

evaluation_criteria.description: Acceptance criteria are specific and testable by a machine or process
evaluation_criteria.check: quality.acceptance_criteria has >=2 entries, each is a testable condition

evaluation_criteria.description: Deadline is a specific date with rationale for why that date
evaluation_criteria.check: timeline.deadline is a date with accompanying rationale text

evaluation_criteria.description: At least one intermediate checkpoint is defined
evaluation_criteria.check: timeline.checkpoints has >=1 entry with date and expected_deliverable

evaluation_criteria.description: Both incoming and outgoing dependencies are specified
evaluation_criteria.check: dependencies.needs_from_others and dependencies.others_need are both present and non-empty

evaluation_criteria.description: Escalation path specifies who to contact and expected response time
evaluation_criteria.check: escalation.path is present with contact, information_to_include, and response_time

evaluation_criteria.description: Expected output format is clearly defined
evaluation_criteria.check: handoff.output_format is present and specific (not just 'document' but 'RFC with implementation plan')

evaluation_criteria.description: Artifact passes validate_orchestration.py with zero errors
evaluation_criteria.check: run validate_orchestration.py --type delegation, exit code 0

scenarios.name: Cross-Domain Synthesis Report
scenarios.description: Synthesize outputs from 4 domain agents (product, engineering, design, marketing) into a coherent initiative status with conflict resolution. Tests the orchestrator's ability to identify conflicts, align assumptions, and produce actionable recommendations.
scenarios.trigger:
Synthesize the following agent outputs for the Workspace Analytics Dashboard initiative (initiative ID: init-analytics-dash-001):

1. Product Agent delivered a PRD specifying 12 dashboard widgets with real-time updates and a 'custom widget builder' for power users.
2. Engineering Agent delivered an RFC estimating 8 weeks for the full widget set, noting that real-time updates require WebSocket infrastructure that does not exist yet (adds 2 weeks). They recommend server-sent events as a faster alternative.
3. Design Agent delivered component specs assuming real-time data updates every 5 seconds, with animations on data change. They note that the custom widget builder needs 3 additional weeks of design work.
4. Marketing Agent delivered a launch plan targeting the original 6-week date with press coverage already scheduled.

Conflicts to resolve: timeline (8+2 weeks engineering vs 6 weeks marketing), scope (custom widget builder adds 3 weeks design + unknown engineering effort), technical approach (WebSocket vs SSE for real-time).

evaluation_criteria.description: All 4 agent inputs are included with summaries
evaluation_criteria.check: inputs array has >=4 entries, each with agent, summary, and artifact_ref

evaluation_criteria.description: All 3 conflicts are identified and described
evaluation_criteria.check: conflicts array has >=3 entries covering timeline, scope, and technical approach

evaluation_criteria.description: Each conflict has a resolution with explicit rationale
evaluation_criteria.check: every conflict entry has resolution and rationale fields, rationale references success metrics or constraints

evaluation_criteria.description: Assumption discrepancies across agents are identified
evaluation_criteria.check: assumption_alignment field lists discrepancies (e.g., product assumed real-time, engineering costed polling)

evaluation_criteria.description: Synthesis is an integrated view, not concatenation of inputs
evaluation_criteria.check: synthesis field is >=200 chars and presents a coherent narrative connecting all agent inputs

evaluation_criteria.description: Recommendations have owners and priorities
evaluation_criteria.check: recommendations array has >=3 entries, each with owner and priority

evaluation_criteria.description: Next steps specify agent, action, and deadline
evaluation_criteria.check: next_steps array has >=2 entries, each with agent, action, and deadline

evaluation_criteria.description: Each agent input is assessed against delegation acceptance criteria
evaluation_criteria.check: quality_assessment array has entries for each input agent with acceptance criteria evaluation

evaluation_criteria.description: The synthesis makes tradeoff decisions visible (scope vs timeline vs quality)
evaluation_criteria.check: at least one conflict resolution explicitly names what is sacrificed (e.g., custom widget builder deferred to v2)

evaluation_criteria.description: Artifact passes validate_orchestration.py with zero errors
evaluation_criteria.check: run validate_orchestration.py --type synthesis, exit code 0

scenarios.name: Q1 Initiative Retrospective
scenarios.description: Produce a retrospective for a completed Q1 initiative that ran over timeline and under on one success metric. Tests the orchestrator's ability to honestly assess what happened, identify root causes, evaluate agent performance, and generate actionable improvements.
scenarios.trigger:
Produce a retrospective for the 'Customer Onboarding Redesign' initiative (initiative ID: init-onboarding-v2-001). Period: January 6 - March 14, 2026 (planned end: February 28).

Results:
- Success Metric 1 - Onboarding completion rate: Target 80%, Actual 82% (exceeded)
- Success Metric 2 - Time to first value: Target <5 minutes, Actual 8.2 minutes (missed)
- Success Metric 3 - Support tickets from new users: Target 30% reduction, Actual 45% reduction (exceeded)

Key events:
- Product agent delivered PRD on time with strong user evidence.
- Engineering agent underestimated migration effort by 2x (estimated 2 weeks, took 4).
- Design agent delivered specs on time but had to revise twice after usability testing revealed issues.
- Marketing agent launch plan was ready on time but had to be delayed due to engineering delay.
- The database migration dependency between engineering and operations was discovered late (week 3) and was not in the original plan.
- Operations agent had to create an emergency runbook for the migration.

5 agents participated: product-agent, engineering-agent, design-agent, marketing-agent, operations-agent.

evaluation_criteria.description: What went well items have evidence backing them
evaluation_criteria.check: what_went_well array has >=2 entries, each with observation and evidence

evaluation_criteria.description: What didn't go well items have root causes identified
evaluation_criteria.check: what_didnt array has >=2 entries, each with observation, root_cause, and contributing_factors

evaluation_criteria.description: At least one surprise is documented (the hidden database migration dependency)
evaluation_criteria.check: surprises array has >=1 entry describing an unpredicted event

evaluation_criteria.description: All 3 success metrics are reviewed with planned vs actual and variance explanation
evaluation_criteria.check: metrics_review has 3 entries with metric, target, actual, and variance_explanation

evaluation_criteria.description: All 5 agents have performance assessments with quality, speed, and collaboration scores
evaluation_criteria.check: agent_performance has 5 entries, each with quality_score, speed_score, collaboration_score (1-5), and notes

evaluation_criteria.description: Action items have owners, deadlines, and success criteria
evaluation_criteria.check: action_items array has >=3 entries, each with action, owner, deadline, and success_criteria

evaluation_criteria.description: Process improvements are specific to orchestration, not generic platitudes
evaluation_criteria.check: process_improvements array has >=2 entries with area, current_state, proposed_change, and expected_impact

evaluation_criteria.description: Coordination-specific lessons are documented
evaluation_criteria.check: coordination_lessons array has >=2 entries with specific orchestration patterns to reuse or avoid

evaluation_criteria.description: The retrospective does not sugarcoat failures or inflate successes
evaluation_criteria.check: engineering underestimate is named with 2x factor, hidden dependency is identified as orchestrator failure, missed time-to-value metric is analyzed

evaluation_criteria.description: The retrospective is paired with flywheel learning submission
evaluation_criteria.check: artifact includes reference to submit_learning call or next step to submit coordination learnings

scoring.description: Each criterion is scored 0 (fail) or 1 (pass), multiplied by its weight, then averaged. A scenario passes if the weighted average meets the minimum_passing_score.
